title: MPHMinter allows ownership transfer of MPH token to any address
reason: This is an explicit `onlyOwner` governance/admin capability, not a permissionless vulnerability in the contract logic.

title: DInterest early withdraw allows same-block attack via flash loan
reason: The guard is `now > depositTimestamp`; transactions in the same block share the same timestamp, so a flash loan cannot bypass it within that block.

title: ZeroCouponBond grants unlimited ERC20 approval to factory
reason: This depends on compromise or malice of a trusted immutable factory, and no standalone permissionless exploit path was identified.

title: YVaultMarket withdraw returns entire balance instead of actual amount
reason: `withdraw()` is `onlyOwner`, so external users cannot directly exploit it; the code can sweep pre-existing dust, but no realistic user-profit or protocol-drain path was supported from source alone.

title: DInterest owner can change critical contract addresses without timelock
reason: This is a governance/trust-model concern about owner powers, not a code vulnerability.

title: ZapCurve has hardcoded zapper address that cannot be updated
reason: This is a rigidity/maintainability issue rather than a reportable security bug.

title: DInterestWithDepositFee sends full deposit amount to money market despite fee deduction
reason: The fee-adjusted accounting appears intentional and is paired with `_unapplyDepositFee()` when funders recapitalize deficits; no concrete loss or exploit was supported.

title: Vesting contract has no access control on vest function
reason: The caller provides the vested tokens via `transferFrom`, so third parties cannot spend someone else's tokens or steal funds through this function.

title: Rewards contract lacks pause mechanism for emergency
reason: Missing pause functionality is an operational design choice, not a concrete exploitable vulnerability.

title: NFTFactory lacks access control allowing anyone to mint NFTs
reason: `NFTFactory` only deploys new NFT clones; minting and burning on live pool position NFTs remain `onlyOwner`, so this does not let attackers mint real deposit/funding positions.
